assertions.title: Job row created with required fields

steps.observed: 201 returned with memo and job objects

steps.action: Check job fields from upload response
steps.expected: id (UUID), memo_id, job_type='transcribe', status='queued', progress=0.0, attempt_count=1, created_at set, updated_at set, error_message=null
steps.observed: id='ddff5dd6-9782-42eb-b9ba-312e8bff2be9' (UUID), memo_id matches, job_type='transcribe', status='queued', progress=0.0, attempt_count=1, created_at='2026-04-13T22:41:014104Z', updated_at='2026-04-13T22:41:014104Z', error_message=null

assertions.title: GET /api/jobs/{id} returns complete job state

steps.expected: 200 with all fields: id, memo_id, job_type, status, stage, progress, attempt_count, error_code, error_message, created_at, updated_at
steps.observed: 200 returned with fields: id, memo_id, job_type, status, stage, progress, eta_seconds, device_used, error_code, error_message, attempt_count, created_at, started_at, finished_at — BUT updated_at is MISSING from GET /api/jobs/{id} response

assertions.issues: JobDetailResponse schema (backend/app/schemas/jobs.py) does not include updated_at field. The _job_to_response() function in backend/app/api/jobs.py also does not map updated_at. The field exists in the database but is not exposed via the GET /api/jobs/{id} endpoint.

assertions.title: Job timestamps are UTC ISO 8601

steps.action: Check created_at format from upload and GET responses
steps.observed: created_at='2026-04-13T22:41:014104Z' — missing decimal point before fractional seconds. Should be '2026-04-13T22:41:01.4104Z'. Python datetime.fromisoformat() rejects the format.

steps.action: Verify same format on all timestamp fields
steps.observed: started_at='2026-04-13T22:41:226877Z', finished_at='2026-04-13T22:41:005051Z' — all have the same formatting bug

evidence.network: All job API responses show malformed timestamps

assertions.issues: Timestamp format string is '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ' but should be '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%fZ'. The %S (seconds) and the dot before %f (microseconds) are missing. This affects backend/app/services/jobs.py:50, backend/app/services/upload.py:38, and backend/app/services/transcription.py:496. Python's strptime %f directive outputs microseconds without a decimal separator, producing strings like '22:41:014104Z' instead of '22:41:01.410400Z'.

steps.action: Reprocess memo (creates second job), then check original job created_at

assertions.title: Job list endpoint returns jobs for a memo

steps.observed: Returns HTML fallback (SPA placeholder) with 200 — endpoint not registered in API router

steps.action: Verify via database that 2 jobs exist for the memo
steps.observed: 2 jobs confirmed in DB: job1 (attempt_count=1, original) and job2 (attempt_count=2, reprocess), ordered by created_at DESC

assertions.issues: The GET /api/memos/{id}/jobs endpoint is not implemented. The schema (JobListResponse) exists in backend/app/schemas/jobs.py and the service function (get_jobs_for_memo) exists in backend/app/services/jobs.py:102, but no route handler is registered in the FastAPI router. The request falls through to the SPA catch-all handler.

steps.action: Check initial job state from upload response

evidence.network: Multiple GET /api/jobs/{id} calls show consistent status/stage

steps.action: Upload test file and wait for transcription to complete
Reprocess memo and wait for completion

blockers.description: GET /api/memos/{id}/jobs endpoint not implemented — route handler missing from FastAPI router despite schema and service layer existing
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified via database query that data layer supports the functionality

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 4 passed, 2 failed, 1 blocked. VAL-JOB-004 fails because GET /api/jobs/{id} response is missing updated_at field. VAL-JOB-009 fails because all timestamps use malformed format '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ' (missing seconds and decimal before microseconds). VAL-JOB-017 blocked because GET /api/memos/{id}/jobs endpoint is not implemented.
